reviewer: hand-authored (Opus) — driven headless-Chrome end-to-end run + desktop+mobile+light/dark screenshots

rubric.evidence: Driven headless run (fresh profile, served copy): clicked Download (~240 MB) → model loaded on WASM → clicked Transcribe on the bundled Russian sample → real Cyrillic transcript 'древнейрусский период началом древнерусского периода принято считать процесс обосабления восточных словян из общиславянского единства и появления первых восточно-славенстог' (549 frames, 155 emitted chars, backend WASM). Console + network clean (0 errors). Source loads AutoProcessor + AutoModelForCTC in a Web Worker and does a real greedy CTC decode over 39-symbol logits — no canned result.
rubric.notes: Also confirmed at the model level directly: onnx-community/wav2vec2-large-xlsr-53-russian-ONNX dtype q4 on WASM returns logits dims [1, T, 39] and, on the real clip, coherent Russian matching the sample's topic (the 'Old Russian period' Spoken-Wikipedia excerpt).

rubric.evidence: Output is a Russian transcript string whose words match the spoken audio (verified against the Spoken-Wikipedia source topic). 'See inside' renders the real per-frame argmax strip (549 Cyrillic/blank cells) and the mechanical collapse (merge duplicates, drop blanks) into the transcript. Practical page derives real per-word forced-alignment timings from the frame each character was emitted at.
rubric.notes: Expected shape (frames × 39-symbol Cyrillic vocab → collapsed text + per-word timings) is correct and legibly surfaced.

rubric.evidence: conformance-derived: declares-model-id (onnx-community/wav2vec2-large-xlsr-53-russian-ONNX linked to card), declares-task (automatic-speech-recognition), declares-backend (WASM/WebGPU), declares-quantisation (q4), inference-off-main-thread (worker.js). At-a-glance table names the XLSR-53 architecture, the 39-symbol Cyrillic vocab, dtype q4 (onnx/model_q4.onnx), and explicitly notes the q8/int8 exports are degenerate so q4 is the honest runnable export.
rubric.notes: Backend/dtype match what worker.js actually loads (device webgpu-if-adapter-else-wasm, dtype q4). Honest, complete disclosure incl. the degenerate-quant caveat.

rubric.evidence: First visit: loader in 'download-required' with a single 'Download model (~240 MB)' button — no silent transfer (verified: no model network fetch before the click). Warm reload in the same profile: loader auto-inits to 'ready' with the ONLY action button being 'Clear cached model' (verified warm_loader.buttons == ['Clear cached model'], no Download). Uses the shared createModelLoader.
rubric.notes: Matches the auto-init contract exactly: absent → labelled Download; cached-current → auto-init, no button; per-model clear-cache present.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region (role=status aria-live present), errors-on-the-page (.err status surface + Retry via loader). Transcribe shows 'Running the CTC model…' then 'Done.'; decode/mic failures set status .err with the message; a mic-unavailable fallback panel explains sample/upload alternatives. runs-without-webgpu-fallback: worker prefers a real WebGPU adapter and falls back to WASM (this run used WASM).
rubric.notes: Progress/ready/error wired accessibly. Offline-cached reuse and a forced partial/evicted cache path were not separately driven this pass (openQuestions).

rubric.evidence: Main page: Record (mic), Upload, Russian sample chip, Transcribe, audio <player> controls. Basics: upload + sample + Transcribe (+ per-frame strip). Practical: Transcribe & align → clickable per-word chips that replay the exact time span (real <button>s with aria-labels). Wild: in-language vs out-of-language sample chips + Transcribe. Multi-model: two loaders + Transcribe & translate. Primary buttons min-block-size:44px; chips 40px.
rubric.notes: controls-operable is manual-evidenced; a full keyboard-only traversal (tab order + visible focus across all 5 pages) is the remaining verification.

rubric.evidence: Before a run the transcript shows only an instructional placeholder and the see-inside panel shows an empty-state message; the loader shows the honest absent-model state on a fresh profile. The visible Cyrillic transcript was produced by the on-device CTC run (549 frames observed).
rubric.notes: No pre-baked result anywhere; the Wild page even shows honest cross-lingual breakdown (Cyrillic gibberish) on English input rather than hiding it.

rubric.evidence: conformance: images-have-alt (canvas waveform + strip carry aria-label/role=img; no bare content ), buttons-have-names (all buttons have text). Status uses role=status aria-live=polite; transcript/collapsed use aria-live=polite; Cyrillic regions marked lang="ru"; per-word replay buttons have descriptive aria-labels.
rubric.notes: Strong baseline. A full screen-reader pass over the streaming transcript and the Cyrillic per-frame grid would further validate.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean. Driven matrix on the main page: scrollWidth<=innerWidth at 1280×800 AND 360×740 (DPR3, touch). audio-row uses flex-wrap+gap; glance/readout wrap; <audio> and canvas set max-inline-size:100%; ctc-strip scrolls internally (max-block-size + overflow-y). Light + dark screenshots read and confirmed.
rubric.notes: Support seeded needs-review by the automated pre-scan; this driven pass is the evidence to flip main-page desktop+mobile to ok. Sub-pages share the same CSS + layout patterns.

rubric.evidence: All inference (CTC forward pass + decode) runs in a Web Worker (worker.js) so the ~315M-param model never blocks the main thread; the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned) to the worker. inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced (off-thread architecture).
rubric.notes: An explicit INP measurement during transcription would confirm; the off-thread transfer is the key mitigation. WASM decode of an 11s clip completed in the headless sandbox with a clean console.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap+gap and intrinsic sizing; set min-inline-size:0 / max-inline-size:100% on flex items and media so long content and wide elements can't force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — audio-row/sample-row/readout use flex-wrap+gap; <audio> and the waveform canvas set max-inline-size:100%; the CTC strip scrolls inside its own overflow-y container; transcript uses overflow-wrap:anywhere. Verified no horizontal overflow at 360×740 and 1280×800.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; driven overflow check true/true; screenshots read

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates aria-live
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state changes in an accessible live region; label every control; keep buttons named and keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live=polite loader/status regions; transcript + collapsed use aria-live=polite; per-word replay buttons carry aria-labels; Cyrillic content marked lang="ru" for correct SR pronunciation.

guidanceConsulted.query: stream/transcribe without INP regressions long tasks
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy/CPU-bound work off the main thread so it doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the CTC forward pass and decode run in models/xlsr-multilingual-asr/worker.js; the 16 kHz audio buffer is transferred to the worker. The main thread only draws the waveform and renders the returned strip.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; driven run stayed responsive; console clean

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible file upload and microphone capture button
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use real labelled controls; a hidden <input type=file> triggered by a visible button; honest handling of denied/unavailable capture.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — Upload is a visible <button> that clicks a hidden accessible file input; the mic uses getUserMedia with a visible record toggle and an explicit no-microphone fallback panel pointing to sample/upload.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: MicRecorder.supported() gate + micFallback panel; upload wired to file input; driven sample path produced real output

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (inherited) — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (shared shell); all pages link the shared stylesheet and crumb back to the catalogue/model page.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; crumbs on every page

openQuestions: Keyboard-only traversal across all 5 pages: is every control reachable in a sensible focus order with a visible focus ring, and do the per-word replay buttons announce and operate correctly?
Drive the partial/evicted cache and offline-cached states explicitly (delete one cached file → expect Re-download; go offline with a warm cache → expect it to still run).
Confirm the WebGPU path on a real GPU device (this pass ran WASM in a no-GPU sandbox) — expect faster decode with identical output.
Measure real INP during the CTC forward pass to confirm the off-thread transfer keeps interactions snappy on a low-end device.

followUpGoals.goal: Keyboard + screen-reader matrix pass for xlsr-multilingual-asr across all 5 pages (focus order, visible focus, SR announcement of the Cyrillic transcript and per-frame grid).
Add sibling XLSR-53 language demos (Turkish, Chinese) from the same backbone to make cross-lingual transfer a comparative family, each a new stable slug.
Add a mobile memory-pressure pre-check before the ~240 MB q4 download on constrained devices.

summary: xlsr-multilingual-asr is a genuinely-distinct, honest demo: wav2vec2-large-XLSR-53 fine-tuned for Russian — a cross-lingual CTC recogniser whose output alphabet is Cyrillic — materially different from the built English wav2vec2 (different backbone pre-training, different language specialisation, different vocab). Verified runnable BEFORE building (logits [1,T,39]; real Russian transcript on the sample clip) and driven end-to-end in headless Chrome: fresh profile shows a labelled ~240 MB Download with no silent transfer, a real Cyrillic transcript (549 frames, 155 chars, WASM), warm reload auto-inits to ready with only a Clear-cache button, clean console, and no horizontal overflow on desktop or mobile. dtype q4 chosen honestly (q8/int8 exports are degenerate). Five pages: basics (transcript + strip), practical (per-word forced alignment with click-to-replay), wild (cross-lingual breakdown on English input), multi-model (XLSR → OPUS-MT ru→en translation). Remaining work is a keyboard/SR traversal and explicit partial/offline cache-state drives. Revision 1.
